summary: Russia is gearing up for a new wave of missile‑drone strikes targeting Ukrainian political and military decision‑making centers, while Ukraine continues intensive drone raids on Russian territory. Heavy Shahed attacks are saturating multiple Ukrainian oblasts, causing civilian casualties and stretching air defenses. Ukrainian special forces and SBS have achieved notable air‑kill successes, including downing a Russian Be‑200 amphibious aircraft.
key_points: Russia plans coordinated missile‑drone attacks on Ukrainian decision‑making hubs.
Ukrainian drones strike deep into Russian regions, hitting oil infrastructure and civilian targets.
Shahed‑type UAVs are being launched from several Ukrainian fronts, increasing pressure on air defenses.
Ukrainian air defenses have shot down dozens of Russian UAVs across multiple oblasts.
Civilian toll rises after a Kyiv missile strike kills 24, including three children.
Ukrainian SBS forces destroyed a Russian Be‑200 amphibious aircraft and other assets.
Finland temporarily closed airspace after unidentified drones entered its airspace.

sections.executive_summary: Russia is preparing a coordinated missile‑drone campaign against Ukrainian decision‑making centers, marking a potential escalation in strategic targeting. Ukraine is sustaining high‑tempo drone operations against Russian infrastructure, while Shahed attacks proliferate across its own territory, straining air defense networks. Ukrainian special forces have achieved notable air‑kill successes, including the downing of a Russian Be‑200, demonstrating continued offensive capability.

points: Zelensky warned that Russia plans new strikes on Ukrainian political and military decision‑making centers, urging intensified defensive measures (Source: @UkraineNow/81239).
Ukrainian forces reported the rescue operation after a Russian missile destroyed a Kyiv apartment block, killing 24 civilians, including three children (Source: @UkraineNow/81207).
Ukrainian SBS units destroyed a Russian Be‑200 amphibious aircraft and damaged a Ka‑27 helicopter, among other targets (Source: @UkraineNow/81214).
Multiple Shahed groups were observed over Poltava, Sumy, Kharkiv, and Chernihiv regions, indicating a broadened UAV offensive (Source: @eRadarrua/91972).
Ukrainian drones were reported over Lithuanian and Latvian airspace, prompting diplomatic protests (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30180).
Russian forces launched attacks on the Ryazan oil refinery and damaged residential buildings, causing civilian casualties (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30185).
Over 40 Russian UAVs were shot down in the Rostov region, and nine in Tula, reflecting heavy aerial losses (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30185).
Russian air defenses intercepted several Ukrainian UAVs targeting the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30185).
Russian missile strikes continue in the Kherson region, with UAV attacks injuring civilians in Brilevka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30185).
Russia is reportedly preparing a massed strike on Ukrainian “centers of decision‑making” as part of a new escalation (Source: @UkraineNow/81247).
Ukrainian assault units of the “North” Army Group advanced up to 1.1 km in the Volchansk sector of Kharkiv, encountering fierce resistance (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30185).
Shahed attacks were reported near Kharkiv airport and in the Dnipro‑Donetsk border area, increasing pressure on eastern defenses (Source: @kpszsu/61974, @kpszsu/61952).
Ukrainian drones targeted the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant perimeter, injuring two plant employees (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30185).
Russian forces reported continued skirmishes in Sumy’s Shostka district and forested areas near Bachivsk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30185).
Ukrainian artillery struck Kamyanka‑Dneprovskaya and Energodar from the AFU side (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30185).
Ukrainian reports of Shahed launches from the Primorsko‑Akhterska and Kursk districts indicate northern UAV activity (Source: @eRadarrua/91939).
Threat of ballistic weapons from the north was noted, suggesting possible long‑range strikes (Source: @kpszsu/61956).
Ukrainian forces observed a UAV near Odessа and the Black Sea region, with local air defense engagement (Source: @kpszsu/61963).
Finnish airspace was temporarily closed after unidentified drones entered, highlighting northern spill‑over risks (Source: @Faytuks_Network/2732).
Ukrainian radar detected a reactive Shahed heading toward Irpin, Bucha, and surrounding Kyiv suburbs (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66746).
Ukrainian UAV attack on a train near Zaporizhzhia caused damage, confirming continued southern strike capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81213).
Russian drone attacks in Kherson’s Aleshkinsky district injured a civilian woman (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30185).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV strikes on the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant area, with injuries to plant staff (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30185).
SBS reported 55 fire engagements across southern operational depth, including in Berdiansk and Melitopol (Source: @UkraineNow/81214).
Ukrainian drones were observed over the Rostov region, with over 40 UAVs destroyed, indicating southern pressure on Russian rear areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30185).
Ukrainian forces downed more than 40 Russian UAVs in Rostov and nine in Tula, showing effective air defense (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30185).
Russian air defenses intercepted Ukrainian Shahed drones over multiple oblasts, including Kharkiv and Poltava (Source: @eRadarrua/91972).
Finnish Air Force scrambled Hornets after drone incursions, temporarily halting Helsinki Airport traffic (Source: @Faytuks_Network/2732).
Ukrainian SBS destroyed a Russian Be‑200 amphibious aircraft and damaged a Ka‑27 helicopter (Source: @UkraineNow/81214).
Ukrainian drones penetrated deep into Russian airspace, striking Ryazan refinery and residential areas, prompting Russian retaliatory strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30185).

key_developments: Russia is preparing a coordinated missile‑drone strike on Ukrainian decision‑making centers, potentially escalating strategic targeting (Source: @UkraineNow/81239).
Ukrainian drones attacked the Ryazan oil refinery and multiple Russian cities, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30185).
Shahed‑type UAVs were launched from several Ukrainian regions, with groups reported in Poltava, Sumy, Kharkiv, and Chernihiv (Source: @eRadarrua/91972).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down over 40 Russian UAVs in Rostov and nine in Tula, demonstrating heightened aerial contest (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30185).
A Russian missile strike on a Kyiv apartment block killed 24 civilians, including three children, intensifying the humanitarian toll (Source: @UkraineNow/81207).
Ukrainian SBS forces destroyed a Russian Be‑200 amphibious aircraft and damaged a Ka‑27 helicopter, marking a significant air‑kill achievement (Source: @UkraineNow/81214).
Finland temporarily closed its airspace after unidentified drones entered, prompting fighter jet scrambles and highlighting regional spill‑over (Source: @Faytuks_Network/2732).
sections.threat_assessment: The imminent Russian missile‑drone campaign against Ukrainian command and control nodes poses a heightened strategic threat, potentially degrading Ukraine's decision‑making capacity. Simultaneously, the proliferation of Shahed UAVs across multiple fronts strains Ukrainian air defenses, increasing the risk of civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. Ukrainian offensive drone operations continue to pressure Russian rear areas, but the growing aerial contest raises the likelihood of escalation.
sections.regional_response: Western partners have reaffirmed support, with 36 countries and the EU establishing a special tribunal committee to address Russian war crimes, signaling sustained diplomatic pressure. Neighboring states like Finland have taken immediate defensive actions against stray drones, reflecting broader regional security concerns. Ukraine's intensified air‑defense posture and SBS successes demonstrate adaptive resilience amid the escalating threat environment.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Russian launch sites for the planned decision‑making center strikes should be prioritized, as well as tracking Shahed supply chains from the north and east. Enhanced radar coverage over Kyiv and other high‑value urban areas is critical to mitigate civilian casualties. Continued assessment of UAV infiltration routes into NATO airspace, exemplified by the Finnish incident, is essential for regional stability.
